head: fc3f0d112005a7248c8d1502f9458bf50b828193

invariant_check.1_dag: pass — invariants::find_cycle / find_cycle_indices (DFS color) run on both validate_subgraph and validate_builder paths. Self-loops, multi-node cycles, deep chains (50-node), and branch-arm cycles all caught per G6 test suite.
invariant_check.2_depth: pass — validate_builder counts CALL primitives on the longest path; InvariantConfig::max_depth default 64; diagnostic longest_path / depth_actual populated.
invariant_check.3_fanout: pass — per-node fan-out = max(explicit outgoing, declared iterate_parallel); enforced at registration.
invariant_check.4_sandbox_nest: deferred-phase-2 — consistent with plan; Phase-2 primitives (SANDBOX) reject at call time via primitives/*::execute returning PrimitiveNotImplemented.
invariant_check.5_node_cap: pass — 4096 default, enforced on both Subgraph and builder-snapshot paths.
invariant_check.8_iter_budget: pass — g6-cag-1 closed at 7927b44: registration-time IterateNestDepth and run-time IterateBudget are now distinct InvariantViolation variants. evaluator.rs:173-175 returns IterateBudget on cumulative-step exhaustion, mapping to E_INV_ITERATE_BUDGET; registration-time nest-depth returns IterateNestDepth → E_INV_ITERATE_NEST_DEPTH. No conflation.
invariant_check.9_determinism: partial — builder path (validate_builder) rejects non-deterministic primitives inside declare_deterministic(true); finalized-Subgraph path (validate_subgraph) silently skips invariant 9 because the determinism flag is builder-only metadata (invariants.rs:137-149, explicit TODO(phase-2)). DSL SubgraphSpec → Subgraph conversion flows through build_unvalidated_for_test and is then validated by Engine::register_subgraph via sg.validate(&cfg), which takes the finalized-Subgraph path — so DSL-authored handlers do NOT get determinism classification enforced at registration. Mirrors g6-cag-4; plan-scoped to Phase 2. Finding r6-cag-2 (minor) below.
invariant_check.10_hash: pass — canonical_subgraph_bytes sorts nodes by (id, kind) and edges by (from, to, label); serde_ipld_dagcbor canonicalizes CBOR map keys. BLAKE3 over canonical bytes. Unit test cid_order_independent_over_edges green. load_verified re-hashes + rejects on mismatch. Pre-R6 mermaid real-wire change did not touch canonicalization.
invariant_check.12_registration_validate: pass — Engine::register_subgraph calls sg.validate(&cfg) before insertion; aggregate-mode via register_subgraph_aggregate populates violated_invariants; DuplicateHandler fires when handler_id reappears with different CID. Idempotent re-registration (same handler_id + same CID) returns the same CID without mutation.
invariant_check.13_immutable: pass — g6-cag-3 closed at 7927b44: Subgraph.nodes / Subgraph.edges / Subgraph.handler_id are now pub(crate) with read-only accessors (nodes() / edges() / handler_id()). first_op_mut / write_op_mut / op_by_id_mut remain pub for the dispatch-time property backfill on ephemeral CRUD subgraphs — these are synthesized at call time inside dispatch_call_inner (never stored back into inner.handlers / inner.specs), so they cannot corrupt any registered subgraph's CID. handlers map only accepts inserts (no remove, no overwrite with different content); specs map mirrors. No mutation path to a registered subgraph reachable from user API.

dispatch_path_audit.claim: Engine::call is the sole dispatch path and routes every handler through the Evaluator — Compromise #8 closed.
dispatch_path_audit.evidence: benten-engine/src/lib.rs:626-631 (Engine::call) → :781-827 (dispatch_call) → :829-950 (dispatch_call_inner). The inner fn builds a Subgraph from either the stored SubgraphSpec (subgraph_for_spec) or the CRUD synthesizer (subgraph_for_crud), then runs `evaluator.run(&subgraph, input_value, self as &dyn PrimitiveHost)` (:865). No CRUD fast-path bypass exists. CRUD ops synthesize an ephemeral READ/WRITE/RESPOND subgraph at dispatch time; the structural shape is engine-authored (not user-authored), so bypassing registration-time invariant 1-6/9-10 validation on the ephemeral is safe. call_as and call_with_revocation_at also funnel to dispatch_call (:641, :655).

view_3_audit.claim: post:list via View 3 does NOT introduce a dispatch path that bypasses invariant checks.
view_3_audit.evidence: `crud:<label>:list` is synthesized by subgraph_for_crud as a READ + RESPOND subgraph (lib.rs:1001-1017); the evaluator walks it end-to-end. Only after the evaluator returns successfully does outcome_from_terminal_with_cid (:2162) call resolve_list_via_view_or_backend (:2237) to materialize the actual Node list from View 3 (content_listing / content_listing_<label>) or the backend label index. The View 3 read is a post-evaluator outcome-materialization step on an already-validated evaluator terminal — not an alternate entry into the engine.

grant_backed_policy_boundary_audit.claim: GrantBackedPolicy fires ONLY at write-commit (the documented check_write boundary); does not smuggle checks into subgraph eval.
grant_backed_policy_boundary_audit.evidence: GrantBackedPolicy::check_write (grant_backed.rs:105-165) called exactly at lib.rs:1448 (inside transaction() with pending PutNode/PutEdge ops gathered from redb's tx callback). The impl PrimitiveHost::check_capability shim (lib.rs:2094-2116) also delegates to policy.check_write on a synthetic ctx, but this fires only if a primitive executor declares a `requires:` scope — Phase-1 executors do not, so this path is dormant (per Phase-1 scope; Phase-2 per-primitive check). GrantBackedPolicy::check_read (grant_backed.rs:167-187) is defined but Engine never invokes check_read anywhere in Phase 1; the Engine::call READ path walks backend / IVM directly. Per CapabilityPolicy::check_read doc: 'Phase 1 default: permit every read.' This is the intended posture — read-side gating is Phase-3 scope with benten-id.
grant_backed_policy_boundary_audit.note: The GrantBackedPolicy read-side impl is dead code at the Engine boundary in Phase 1. It exists for policy authors who compose their own surface, and documents compromise #2 Option A. Not a defect — but see finding r6-cag-1 (info) below.

handler_to_mermaid_audit.claim: handler_to_mermaid delegates to benten_eval::diag::mermaid::render; preserves the subgraph-as-data-artifact posture.
handler_to_mermaid_audit.evidence: Engine::handler_to_mermaid (lib.rs:717-754) reconstructs the Subgraph (from cached SubgraphSpec for DSL handlers, or subgraph_for_crud(label, 'create', ...) for zero-config) and calls benten_eval::diag::mermaid::render(&subgraph) (:753). The renderer (diag/mermaid.rs:21-50) walks sg.nodes and sg.edges — pure structural traversal, no interpretation of properties as code. Output starts with `flowchart TD`, contains one line per OperationNode (as `node_id(["KIND: id"])`) and one per edge. The Subgraph IS the Mermaid source; no source-string-stored-as-property path.

findings.claim: GrantBackedPolicy::check_read is defined and overrides the default-permit behaviour with DeniedRead semantics, but the Engine never calls check_read anywhere in Phase 1. The override is dead code at the Engine boundary.
findings.detail: Engine::call for `list` / `get` ops walks backend.get_by_label / backend.get_node / resolve_list_via_view_or_backend directly, never consulting policy.check_read. This matches the documented Phase-1 posture ('permit every read' per CapabilityPolicy::check_read doc; compromise #2 Option A is about the error-code surface, not about enforcement being wired). However, a user who configures GrantBackedPolicy today expecting list/get to honour read-side grants will see targeted reads silently permitted. This is a documentation-vs-behaviour gap more than a code defect.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) call engine's configured policy.check_read from dispatch_call_inner before the list/get path fires, honouring compromise #2's DeniedRead code at the Outcome boundary, or (b) add an explicit Phase-1 note on Engine::call / Engine::read_view rustdoc stating 'read-side capability checks are Phase-3 scope; configured check_read impls will not be consulted in Phase 1.' (b) is the lower-cost disposition and keeps the Phase-1 posture coherent.

findings.claim: DSL-authored SubgraphSpec handlers have invariant 9 (determinism classification) silently skipped at registration because Engine::register_subgraph takes the finalized-Subgraph validation path, not the builder-snapshot path.
findings.detail: IntoSubgraphSpec::into_eval_subgraph (lib.rs:2848-2881) constructs via SubgraphBuilder and terminates with build_unvalidated_for_test(). register_subgraph then calls sg.validate(&cfg), which dispatches to validate_subgraph — the path whose TODO at invariants.rs:142-148 notes 'validation on finalized Subgraph has no determinism flag'. The builder-only metadata (deterministic flag, parallel-fanout annotation, iterate-nest depth) is therefore lost on the DSL path. This is a pre-existing G6 finding (g6-cag-4) flagged as Phase-2 scope and documented in the TODO; the note here is that the actual DSL dispatch path in Phase 1 IS the finalized path, so determinism is genuinely unenforced for DSL handlers today. Proptest `prop_subgraph_cid_order_independent` is also still a `todo!()` stub (g6-cag-2 / .../tests/proptests_subgraph_order.rs:64-66); unit-level order-independence is pinned but fuzz-level is not.
findings.suggested_fix: Phase-2 work to include the determinism flag in the Subgraph's DAG-CBOR canonical shape (so it's part of the CID and round-trips through storage), plus a validate_subgraph_with_flags(sg, &cfg, deterministic: bool) extension the DSL can call before finalizing. No Phase-1 code change required; file a Phase-2 ticket referencing this finding and g6-cag-4.

findings.claim: TRANSFORM's `expr` property carries the expression source as a Text Value and is parsed on every call; the module-level doc claims 'Registration-time validation parses the expression once' but the invariant validator does not invoke the parser.
findings.detail: transform.rs:3-8 documents 'Registration-time validation parses the expression once (via crate::expr::parser::parse); runtime calls walk the AST.' In practice, execute() parses on every call (:41), and invariants.rs makes no call to expr::parser::parse for any PrimitiveKind::Transform node. The per-call parse does not break the code-as-graph contract — the expression language has a bounded grammar enforced by parse() itself, so this is not 'arbitrary source code in a property' — but the promised registration-time parse (which would let callers catch E_TRANSFORM_SYNTAX at register rather than call) is missing. This is a DX gap and a minor correctness concern if a subgraph registers successfully but fails at every call.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a TRANSFORM-specific check in invariants::validate_subgraph / validate_builder that parses each TRANSFORM node's `expr` property once and rejects the registration with InvariantViolation::TransformSyntax (or re-use the existing catch-all) if parse fails. Parse result does not need to be cached (the per-call parse cost is minor), but the failure surface shifts to registration time where it belongs. Document the expr grammar in invariants.rs alongside the existing DAG/depth/fan-out checks.

findings.claim: The specs-map insert path does not mirror the handlers-map duplicate-content guard; re-registering a SubgraphSpec with the same handler_id but structurally equivalent-yet-different content would silently overwrite the stored spec.
findings.detail: register_subgraph's handlers-map guard (lib.rs:490-500) returns DuplicateHandler when the handler_id reappears with a different CID. But the specs-map insert at lib.rs:504 fires unconditionally after that guard clears — so in the idempotent path (same handler_id + same CID), it overwrites with an identical value (safe), and in the DuplicateHandler path the function has already errored before reaching the specs insert. So the observable behaviour is actually correct, but the code reads as if the spec insert could drift from the handlers-map truth under future refactor. Defense-in-depth minor.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) move the spec insert into the match arm that handles the fresh-insert case (consolidating the two maps' update under a single logical event), or (b) add a debug_assert_eq on the existing spec entry when the handler_id was already known. Either keeps the invariant visible in code.

findings.claim: Subgraph retains pub mutators (first_op_mut / write_op_mut / op_by_id_mut) used by benten-engine's dispatch to backfill properties on synthesized CRUD subgraphs. Combined with build_unvalidated_for_test being public, any downstream crate can construct a Subgraph and mutate its nodes without registration — the type system does not currently prevent an unvalidated Subgraph from reaching evaluator.run.
findings.detail: g6-cag-3 closed the Subgraph.nodes / Subgraph.edges visibility; the accessors that remain are pub fn that return &mut OperationNode, so a caller cannot resize the nodes/edges vecs but can edit properties. For the dispatch path this is safe (the structural shape — invariants 1/3/5/6 — is preserved), but invariant 10's CID changes silently when properties mutate. The engine never re-computes or relies on the ephemeral subgraph's CID (the registered handler_cid is tracked separately in inner.handlers), so no user-visible bug — but a future caller who relies on sg.cid() after calling first_op_mut would silently get drift. The safety note in first_op_mut's doc (:495-498) acknowledges this.
findings.suggested_fix: Phase-2 refactor options: (a) replace the property-backfill pattern with a SubgraphBuilder method that sets properties during construction, eliminating the mut accessors, or (b) make first_op_mut / write_op_mut / op_by_id_mut pub(crate) and expose a trait-based property-binding helper to benten-engine only. (a) is cleaner; (b) preserves the current dispatch shape.

notes: Phase 1 ships the code-as-graph contract cleanly. Handlers are subgraphs, not source strings. The evaluator is the sole dispatch path (Compromise #8 closed at the PrimitiveHost landing). The 8 Phase-1 invariants are enforced at registration; the 6 Phase-2 invariants fail loudly at call time or are explicitly skipped with TODO markers. CID is order-independent and the pre-R6 real-mermaid wire did not touch canonicalization. GrantBackedPolicy fires exclusively at write-commit; no back-door. View 3 is a post-evaluator outcome-materialization step, not an alternate dispatch path. All 3 prior G6 findings (g6-cag-1 budget-exhaustion, g6-cag-3 field visibility, g6-cag-7 result_large_err) are verified closed at 7927b44. No critical or major findings at R6; 3 minor + 2 info. Verdict: pass.
